title: 有翼之暗
summary:
McArthur presents a theory regarding the series of murders, beginning with the death of Ito. He asserts that Okumura committed the murder with an accomplice who provided an alibi. According to McArthur, Okumura lied about the order in which the head and body were discovered to make the accomplice's alibi appear fortuitous. He explains that Okumura killed and decapitated Ito on the first floor while the accomplice remained on the second floor. To move the head upstairs, Mukai threw it from the first-floor garden into a second-floor room. The head was painted with white lead powder to disguise bloodstains from the throwing process and to create confusion. McArthur identifies Kanai as the accomplice, noting that Kanai invited Kumagai into the house and used him to deceive the witness, Kagayama.

McArthur further claims that Kumagai killed Shima, as Kumagai could enter the house without suspicion. He also accuses Jiban of being responsible for the subsequent twin murders and the death of Yūko, suggesting Jiban acted on behalf of Wakasugi to avoid suspicion. He posits that Kanai's motive was to seize control of the Mikage family's power by eliminating obstacles, including Ito, Uma, Akiyoshi, Kizuki, Mukumizu, and the twins.

Finally, McArthur connects the case to a historical mystery involving Medvedev and the Inamura family record, "Imakagami." He suggests that Medvedev may have been killed to embezzle hidden Romanov family funds entrusted to him and Tomonori Motomu, implying this historical theft may be linked to the current events and Kumagai.
